agendas.label: Approve rights issue
agendas.description: Shareholders review a proposed rights issue structure and the funding rationale.

host_intro_lines: The chair opens the extraordinary meeting and frames the rights issue as a funding decision for the company’s next phase.
Management steps to the podium and argues that the rights issue is meant to stabilize the balance sheet and extend operational runway.
meeting_presentation.observer_copy: You can watch the room, but only shareholders recorded before the meeting can cast a weighted vote.
meeting_presentation.vote_prompt: The host now asks shareholders to vote on the proposed rights issue agenda.
meeting_presentation.approved_result_copy: The room approves the rights issue proposal. The market will only digest that outcome on the next simulation day.
meeting_presentation.rejected_result_copy: The room rejects the rights issue proposal. The market will only digest that outcome on the next simulation day.

agendas.label: Approve buyback authorization
agendas.description: Shareholders review a proposed buyback mandate and confidence-support rationale.

host_intro_lines: The chair opens the extraordinary meeting and frames the buyback as a capital-return and liquidity-support mandate.
Management says the proposed authorization gives the company room to absorb float, but shareholders are weighing price support against cash discipline.
meeting_presentation.observer_copy: You can watch the room, but only shareholders recorded before the meeting can cast a weighted vote.
meeting_presentation.vote_prompt: The host now asks shareholders to vote on the proposed stock buyback authorization.
meeting_presentation.approved_result_copy: The room approves the buyback authorization. The market will digest the mandate and execution size on the next simulation day.
meeting_presentation.rejected_result_copy: The room rejects the buyback authorization. The market will react to the failed capital-return signal on the next simulation day.

agendas.label: Review tender offer
agendas.description: Shareholders review a controller or strategic investor tender offer and the implied float impact.

host_intro_lines: The chair opens the extraordinary meeting and frames the tender offer as a shareholder election rather than a normal approval vote.
The offeror's terms are on the screen: shareholders can tender into the bid, hold their shares, or simply observe the final acceptance read.
meeting_presentation.observer_copy: You can watch the room, but only shareholders recorded before the meeting can submit an election.
meeting_presentation.vote_prompt: Choose how you want to respond to the tender offer for your record-date position.
meeting_presentation.approved_result_copy: The tender election closes. Your chosen response will feed the next simulation day, while the offeror's public acceptance read still drives float and price effects.
meeting_presentation.rejected_result_copy: The tender election closes with weak support. The market will react to the failed offer on the next simulation day.

agendas.label: Approve stock split
agendas.description: Shareholders review a proposed stock split intended to widen accessibility and liquidity.

host_intro_lines: The chair opens the extraordinary meeting and frames the split proposal as a share-structure and trading-accessibility decision.
Management says the ratio is meant to reset the board lot economics, while shareholders watch whether the market reads it as liquidity support or cosmetic engineering.
meeting_presentation.observer_copy: You can watch the room, but only shareholders recorded before the meeting can cast a weighted vote.
meeting_presentation.vote_prompt: The host now asks shareholders to vote on the proposed stock split or reverse-split terms.
meeting_presentation.approved_result_copy: The room approves the split terms. The market will digest the adjusted shares and reference price on the next simulation day.
meeting_presentation.rejected_result_copy: The room rejects the split terms. The market will react to the failed share-structure proposal on the next simulation day.

agendas.label: Approve CEO / board changes
agendas.description: Shareholders review proposed leadership changes and the stated turnaround rationale.

host_intro_lines: The chair opens the extraordinary meeting and frames the agenda as a governance and execution-reset decision.
The board presents the incoming CEO mandate, transition rationale, and expected operating priorities.
meeting_presentation.observer_copy: You can watch the room, but only shareholders recorded before the meeting can cast a weighted vote.
meeting_presentation.vote_prompt: The host now asks shareholders to vote on the proposed CEO and board-change slate.
meeting_presentation.approved_result_copy: The room approves the leadership slate. The market will digest the CEO transition and execution mandate on the next simulation day.
meeting_presentation.rejected_result_copy: The room rejects the leadership slate. The market will react to the failed governance reset on the next simulation day.

agendas.label: Approve private placement
agendas.description: Shareholders review a targeted placement structure and proposed strategic investor entry.

host_intro_lines: The chair opens the extraordinary meeting and frames the private placement as a fast capital-raising decision.
Management says the placement brings in a targeted investor, but shareholders are watching the dilution and discount closely.
meeting_presentation.observer_copy: You can watch the room, but only shareholders recorded before the meeting can cast a weighted vote.
meeting_presentation.vote_prompt: The host now asks shareholders to vote on the proposed private placement agenda.
meeting_presentation.approved_result_copy: The room approves the private placement proposal. The market will digest the dilution and proceeds on the next simulation day.
meeting_presentation.rejected_result_copy: The room rejects the private placement proposal. The market will react to the failed capital-raising attempt on the next simulation day.

agendas.label: Approve restructuring plan
agendas.description: Shareholders review a restructuring package, asset actions, and leadership commitments.

host_intro_lines: The chair opens the extraordinary meeting and frames the plan as a balance-sheet survival package.
Management says creditors, asset buyers, and shareholders must align before the company can regain operating room.
meeting_presentation.observer_copy: You can watch the room, but only shareholders recorded before the meeting can cast a weighted vote.
meeting_presentation.vote_prompt: The host now asks shareholders to vote on the proposed restructuring plan.
meeting_presentation.approved_result_copy: The room approves the restructuring plan. The market will digest the dilution, debt relief, and asset-sale read on the next simulation day.
meeting_presentation.rejected_result_copy: The room rejects the restructuring plan. The market will react to the failed rescue package on the next simulation day.

agendas.label: Approve strategic M&A
agendas.description: Shareholders review a strategic merger or acquisition proposal and the expected business fit.

host_intro_lines: The chair opens the extraordinary meeting and frames the acquisition as a cash-out decision for minority shareholders.
Management walks through the strategic buyer, premium, and closing risk while the room weighs certain cash against staying listed.
meeting_presentation.observer_copy: You can watch the room, but only shareholders recorded before the meeting can cast a weighted vote.
meeting_presentation.vote_prompt: The host now asks shareholders to vote on the proposed strategic acquisition and cash-out terms.
meeting_presentation.approved_result_copy: The room approves the acquisition proposal. The market will digest the cash-out and delisting result on the next simulation day.
meeting_presentation.rejected_result_copy: The room rejects the acquisition proposal. The market will react to the failed strategic deal on the next simulation day.

agendas.label: Approve change of control
agendas.description: Shareholders review a control change and proposed strategic redirection.

agendas.label: Approve asset injection
agendas.description: Shareholders review a proposed asset injection and new business-line transformation.

host_intro_lines: The chair opens the extraordinary meeting and frames the agenda as a control-change and asset-injection decision.
Management walks through the incoming sponsor, injected asset, new share issuance, dilution, and post-deal identity reset.
meeting_presentation.observer_copy: You can watch the room, but only shareholders recorded before the meeting can cast a weighted vote.
meeting_presentation.vote_prompt: The host now asks shareholders to vote on the proposed control change and asset injection.
meeting_presentation.approved_result_copy: The room approves the backdoor listing proposal. The market will digest the identity reset, dilution, and sponsor lock-up on the next simulation day.
meeting_presentation.rejected_result_copy: The room rejects the backdoor listing proposal. The market will react to the failed asset-injection story on the next simulation day.
